title: 10 best herbal creams for motion sickness

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Peppermint creams helps with motion sickness because of its natural ability to soothe digestive issues, ease nausea, and calm the stomach. The menthol in peppermint oil relaxes the muscles in the stomach, reducing inflammation and cramps that can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms. Additionally, peppermint's calming effect on the nervous system helps to reduce anxiety and stress related to travel, further alleviating discomfort caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Menthol: Menthol is known to stimulate the digestive system and ease stomach discomfort, which can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and vomiting.

Peppermint oil: The oil extracted from peppermint leaves has natural anti-inflammatory properties that help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like bloating and cramps.

Vitamin A: Peppermint cream often contains vitamin A, a nutrient that helps to maintain the health of mucous membranes in the digestive system, which can become irritated during periods of motion sickness, leading to discomfort and nausea.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in volatile oils, peppermint roots are effective in soothing stomach upset and calming digestive issues caused by motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: The thick, underground stems of peppermint plants contain high amounts of menthol, which helps to ease nausea and relieve vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh peppermint leaves from a trusted source in the morning when they are at their peak potency.
Dry the harvested peppermint leaves using a food dehydrator set at 85°f for 6 hours or until crispy.
Combine 100g of dried peppermint leaves with 20g of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat gently for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth into a clean glass container discarding the solids after 10 minutes.
Mix the infused oil with 200g of beeswax and 100g of shea butter melting them together at 140°f until smooth and consistent.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Ginger creams helps with motion sickness because they contain bioactive compounds that stimulate the body's natural response to nausea. The creams' active ingredients, such as gingerol and shogaol, bind to receptors in the stomach and intestines, helping to slow down digestive contractions and reduce feelings of queasiness. This calming effect can alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, allowing individuals to feel more comfortable and relaxed during travel.
remedy_properties: Gingerols: Gingerols, a compound found in ginger, have been shown to reduce nausea and vomiting by inhibiting the release of neurotransmitters that trigger these symptoms, helping to alleviate motion sickness.

Shogaols: Shogaols, another bioactive compound present in ginger, have anti-inflammatory properties that can help relieve nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness, while also reducing stomach upset.

Volatile oils: The volatile oils present in herbal ginger creams, such as citral and geraniol, have natural sedative and anxiolytic effects, which can help calm the body and mind, reducing feelings of anxiety and nausea that often accompany motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the ginger plant are used because they contain bioactive compounds like gingerol and shogaol, which have anti-inflammatory properties that help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Rhyzomes: Ginger rhyzomes are used due to their high concentration of active ingredients like gingerols and sesquiterpenes, which have been shown to be effective in reducing symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Grind 2 tablespoons of dried ginger root into a fine powder using a coffee grinder or mortar and pestle.
Mix the ground ginger powder with 4 ounces of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 15 minutes.
Add 2 drops of lavender essential oil to the mixture and stir well to combine ingredients.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to room temperature, then whip it using an electric mixer for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to a glass jar and store it in the refrigerator for up to 2 weeks before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: English lavender creams helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique blend of natural ingredients that work synergistically to alleviate symptoms. The calming properties of lavender essential oil help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea, while other herbal extracts in the cream, such as peppermint and ginger, help to relax the digestive system and calm the mind. This combination helps to reduce feelings of queasiness and discomfort associated with motion sickness, promoting a sense of relief and relaxation.
remedy_properties: Lavender's calming and sedative effects: Lavender's aromatic compounds, such as linalool and linalyl acetate, have a calming effect on the nervous system, which helps to reduce anxiety and stress related to motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Lavender contains anti-inflammatory compounds like apigenin and linarin, which can help alleviate inflammation in the body that may contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as nausea and dizziness.

Antispasmodic effects: Lavender's antispasmodic properties can help relax the muscles and smooth muscle fibers in the digestive tract, which can reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help soothe digestive issues and alleviate nausea associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: Containing essential oils like linalool and camphor, which have been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality dried lavandula angustifolia flowers in amounts sufficient for the desired quantity of cream.
Combine one part dried lavender flowers with two parts carrier oil such as sweet almond or coconut oil in a double boiler.
Heat the mixture at 160°f to 180°f for 30 minutes, allowing the essential oils to infuse into the carrier oil.
Strain the infused oil through a cheesecloth or coffee filter into a clean glass container, discarding the solids.
Mix 20% of the infused oil with 80% petroleum jelly or beeswax in a heat-proof bowl, melting the mixture at 120°f to 140°f for 10 minutes before pouring into jars.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Chamomile creams helps with motion sickness because of its natural soothing properties that calm the nervous system and reduce anxiety. The active compounds in chamomile, such as apigenin and luteolin, have a sedative effect on the body, which can help to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness like dizziness, nausea, and vomiting. When applied topically, chamomile creams can also cool and comfort the skin, reducing inflammation and discomfort caused by repeated exposure to motion. This natural remedy provides relief from motion sickness without the need for harsh chemicals or medications.
remedy_properties: Apigenin: This flavonoid compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea, vomiting, and dizziness.
Chamazulene: This sesquiterpene lactone has potent anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce inflammation and irritation in the digestive tract, which is often exacerbated by motion sickness.
Luteolin: As a flavonoid, luteolin has antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects that can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by motion sickness, reducing its severity.
remedy_parts: Flowers: Chamomile flowers have a calming effect on the body, which helps to soothe and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Roots: The roots of the chamomile plant contain apigenin, an anti-inflammatory compound that can help reduce nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Measure out one cup of distilled water and bring it to a boil in a double boiler.
Combine two tablespoons of dried matricaria chamomilla flowers with the boiling water and let steep for 10 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a cheesecloth or fine-mesh sieve into a clean glass bowl and discard solids.
Add one tablespoon of coconut oil and four ounces of beeswax to the liquid and stir until melted and fully incorporated.
Pour the mixture into sterilized glass jars and let cool at room temperature for 30 minutes before capping.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Feverfew creams helps with motion sickness because they contain parthenolide, a natural compound that has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties. When applied topically to the skin, feverfew cream can help to reduce inflammation and swelling in the body, which are often caused by motion sickness. This can help to alleviate symptoms such as nausea, dizziness, and headaches associated with motion sickness. Additionally, feverfew's calming effects can also help to soothe the stomach and digestive system, further reducing the discomfort caused by motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Parthenolide: Feverfew's main active compound, parthenolide, has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic effects, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Feverfew's sesquiterpene lactones: These compounds have been shown to exhibit antihistaminic and anti-inflammatory properties, which can help reduce the symptoms of motion sickness by blocking histamine receptors in the brain and reducing inflammation.

Feverfew's flavonoids: Feverfew's flavonoids, such as apigenin and luteolin, have been found to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which can help protect against oxidative stress and inflammation caused by motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in sesquiterpenes, which help to reduce inflammation and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Buds: Containing parthenolide, a compound that has anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties, making it effective against nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 10 grams of fresh tanacetum parthenium leaves from a trusted source for processing.
Dry the harvested leaves in a well-ventilated area at room temperature for 7 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried tanacetum parthenium leaves with 100 grams of coconut oil in a glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for 14 days, shaking daily to ensure thorough infusion.
Strain and filter the mixture through cheesecloth into another glass container, discarding solids and using resulting liquid as cream base.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Valerian creams helps with motion sickness because of its natural sedative properties, which can calm the digestive system and reduce nausea. The cream absorbs into the skin and transdermally delivers valerenic acid to the bloodstream, where it can help alleviate symptoms such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and queasiness. By promoting relaxation and reducing anxiety, herbal valerian creams provide a soothing effect that can greatly alleviate motion sickness discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy their travel or activities without distress.
remedy_properties: Sedative and anxiolytic properties: Valerian's ability to calm the nervous system helps reduce anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms.
Muscle relaxant properties: Valerian's muscle-relaxing effects can alleviate nausea, vomiting, and dizziness associated with motion sickness by reducing muscle tension and spasms.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Valerian's anti-inflammatory properties may help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms such as stomach upset and queasiness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Valerian roots contain valerenic acid, which has anti-inflammatory and sedative properties, making them effective in calming motion sickness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Valerian rhyzomes have been traditionally used to treat digestive issues, including nausea and vomiting, common symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh valeriana officinalis roots dig up mature plants with healthy white roots.
Dry the valeriana officinalis roots air dry or use a food dehydrator for 24 hours.
Grind the dried valeriana officinalis roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder and strain through a fine-mesh sieve.
Mix 200g of the ground valeriana officinalis with 100g of beeswax and 50g of coconut oil in a double boiler over low heat for 20 minutes.
Remove from heat stir in 10ml of sweet almond oil and pour into sterilized glass jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Bitter orange creams helps with motion sickness because they contain bergamot, a citrus essential oil that has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When consumed before traveling or engaging in activities that cause motion sickness, bergamot can help reduce nausea and dizziness by calming the stomach and digestive system. Additionally, bitter orange creams often include other herbs like ginger and peppermint, which have also been known to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Citrus Aurantium: The peel of the bitter orange fruit, also known as Citrus aurantium, contains synephrine and other alkaloids that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness by stimulating digestive processes and improving blood circulation.
Peppermint Oil: Peppermint oil has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea associated with motion sickness.
Ginger: Ginger has natural anti-emetic properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting caused by motion sickness, as well as alleviate symptoms such as dizziness and lightheadedness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots are used to create a calming and sedative effect in the creams, helping to reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
Seeds: The seeds contain alkaloids that help to stimulate digestion and relieve symptoms of motion sickness such as bloating and cramps.
remedy_recipe: Grate 100g of dried citrus x aurantium peel using a cheese grater or microplane into a heat-proof bowl.
Add 50ml of carrier oil such as sweet almond oil or coconut oil and stir to combine in the bowl.
Heat the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the zest is fully infused into the oil.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter or cheesecloth into another bowl, discarding the solids and reserving the infused oil.
Allow the infused oil to cool before whipping it with 20% beeswax and 1% vitamin e oil to create a smooth cream that can be stored in an airtight container for up to 6 months at room temperature.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maypop creams helps with motion sickness because they contain active compounds like magnoflorine, which have natural anti-emetic properties. When applied topically to the skin, these compounds can help to calm the nervous system and reduce feelings of nausea and dizziness associated with motion sickness. The cream's soothing and calming effects on the skin also help to relax tense muscles and improve blood circulation, further alleviating symptoms of motion sickness and promoting a sense of balance and well-being.
remedy_properties: Passionflower extract: Passionflower has been traditionally used to treat anxiety and insomnia, which are often linked to motion sickness. Its sedative and anti-anxiolytic properties can help calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.

Valerian root extract: Valerian root is known for its calming and relaxing effects on the nervous system, which can help alleviate feelings of queasiness and dizziness associated with motion sickness.
Ginger essential oil: Ginger has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help soothe an upset stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting symptoms commonly experienced during motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maypop plant are rich in calming compounds that help to soothe and stabilize the nervous system, reducing symptoms of motion sickness.
Seeds: Maypop seeds contain natural oils that have anti-inflammatory properties, which can help to reduce nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried passionflower herb by collecting flowers and leaves from certified organic sources or cultivating your own plant for 2 weeks.
Crush the dried passionflower into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle for 10 minutes.
Combine 20 grams of crushed passionflower powder with 100 milliliters of coconut oil in a double boiler and heat for 30 minutes at 70 degrees celsius.
Strain the mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass jar and discard solids for 15 minutes.
Allow the cream to cool and solidify completely before transferring it to an airtight container and storing in the refrigerator for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemongrass creams helps with motion sickness because of its calming and soothing properties. The herbal ingredient in lemongrass has a natural ability to reduce nausea and vomiting, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, lemongrass oil has been known to ease digestive issues and promote relaxation, both of which can help alleviate the discomfort associated with motion sickness. When applied topically to the skin as a cream or oil, lemongrass can provide quick relief from nausea and discomfort, allowing individuals to enjoy their travel or outdoor activities without interruption.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Lemongrass contains antioxidants like flavonoids and phenolic acids, which help to neutralize free radicals in the body caused by motion sickness, reducing oxidative stress and inflammation.

Anti-inflammatory properties: The anti-inflammatory compounds in lemongrass, such as citral and geraniol, can help to reduce swelling and pain associated with motion sickness, providing relief from symptoms like headaches and nausea.

Gastroprotective properties: Lemongrass contains compounds that have been shown to protect the stomach lining and reduce acid secretion, which helps to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as stomach upset, nausea, and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in bioactive compounds, lemongrass roots help soothe stomach discomfort and nausea associated with motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: As a natural anti-inflammatory agent, lemongrass rhyzomes reduce swelling and pain caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 20g of fresh cymbopogon citratus leaves, rinse them gently with distilled water and pat dry.
Blend the leaves in a food processor with 50ml of carrier oil such as coconut or sweet almond oil at room temperature.
Combine the blended mixture with 10% beeswax and 5g of vitamin e oil, then heat it to 60°c for 30 minutes.
Strain the mixture through a coffee filter to remove any solids, then pour it into sterilized jars and let it cool.
Store the cream in an airtight container at room temperature, allowing it to mature for 14 days before use.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Kansas coneflower creams helps with motion sickness because they contain the natural herb Echinacea, which has been shown to reduce inflammation and soothe digestive issues. The cream's anti-inflammatory properties help alleviate the discomfort caused by motion sickness, such as dizziness and nausea. Additionally, the calming effects of the cream can help regulate the body's response to motion, reducing feelings of queasiness and improving overall well-being during travel or other activities that may trigger motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: The cream's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as rosinic acid and chicoric acid, help to reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Antioxidant properties: Kansas coneflower's antioxidant compounds, including quercetin and kaempferol, help to neutralize free radicals that can exacerbate motion sickness and reduce oxidative stress.
Calming and anti-nausea properties: The cream's calming agents, such as ursolic acid and oleanolic acid, may help to soothe the stomach and reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Kansas coneflower contain triterpenoid saponins, which have anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties that can help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.

Barks: The barks of the Kansas coneflower contain flavonoids, which have been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, making it useful in reducing nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality echinacea angustifolia roots, ensuring they are free of contaminants and pesticides.
Dry the harvested roots in a warm, well-ventilated area or using a food dehydrator on low heat for 8 hours.
Grind the dried roots into a fine powder using a spice grinder or mortar and pestle, achieving a consistency similar to matcha green tea powder.
Combine 20 grams of echinacea angustifolia root powder with 100 grams of coconut oil and 50 grams of beeswax in a double boiler, heating the mixture at 150°f for 30 minutes.
Strain the cooled cream through cheesecloth or a coffee filter into clean glass jars, filling each jar to within 1/4 inch of the top before sealing tightly for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Maidenhair tree creams helps with motion sickness because they contain a unique blend of herbal extracts that work together to reduce nausea and vomiting. The cream's calming properties help to soothe the stomach and alleviate feelings of discomfort, while its natural antispasmodic agents help to relax muscles and reduce any tension or stress caused by motion sickness. By addressing these physical and emotional symptoms, maidenhair tree creams provide effective relief from the distressing effects of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant and anti-inflammatory properties: Maidenhair fern extract has been shown to have antioxidant and anti-inflammatory effects, which may help reduce nausea and discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Calming and soothing effects: The extract has been used in traditional medicine to calm nervous disorders, including anxiety and stress, which can exacerbate motion sickness symptoms. Its calming properties may also help reduce feelings of queasiness.

Digestive health benefits: Some studies suggest that Maidenhair fern extract may have digestive benefits, such as soothing gastrointestinal issues and reducing inflammation. Motion sickness often causes stomach upset, so this property could potentially alleviate those symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the Maidenhair tree contain a unique compound that helps to calm the nervous system and reduce symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of the Maidenhair tree have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting, making it a valuable component in medicinal creams for motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Sterilize glass jars and utensils by autoclaving them at 15 pounds of pressure for 20 minutes.
Combine 20% ginkgo biloba extract, 10% aloe vera gel, and 70% coconut oil in a mixing bowl.
Heat the mixture gently over low heat for 10-15 minutes or until it reaches a smooth consistency.
Fill sterilized glass jars with the prepared cream to a depth of about 1 inch and seal them tightly.
Allow the creams to cool at room temperature for 2 hours before storing them in a dark, cool place for up to 6 months.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hops creams helps with motion sickness because they contain natural compounds that have a calming effect on the digestive system, reducing nausea and stomach discomfort. The alpha acids present in hops have anti-inflammatory properties, which help to alleviate symptoms of vertigo and dizziness caused by motion sickness. Additionally, hops creams can also help to reduce anxiety and stress, further contributing to their effectiveness in alleviating motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Antiemetic and anti-inflammatory compounds: Hops contain compounds like xanthohumol, flavonoids, and essential oils that have been shown to reduce nausea, vomiting, and inflammation associated with motion sickness.

Sedative and anxiolytic effects: The sedative properties of hops can help calm the nervous system, reducing anxiety and stress related to motion sickness, while also promoting relaxation and reducing discomfort.

Muscle-relaxing and anti-spasmodic properties: Hops' ability to relax muscles and reduce spasms can help alleviate symptoms such as stomach cramps, diarrhea, and dizziness commonly associated with motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in hop resins and essential oils, roots provide a calming and anti-inflammatory effect that helps alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Bark: Containing tannins and other compounds, the bark of the hops plant has been traditionally used to treat digestive issues and nausea associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest high-quality humulus lupulus leaves and flowers according to botanical guidelines.
Dry the plant material in a well-ventilated area at 30°c for 14 days.
Combine 20 grams of dried humulus lupulus leaves and flowers with 100 milliliters of sweet almond oil in a double boiler.
Steep the mixture for 60 minutes, then strain through cheesecloth and discard solids.
Add 10% beeswax and 1% vitamin e oil to the infused oil, stir until melted and pour into jars for storage.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Angelica creams helps with motion sickness because they contain essential oils derived from the angelica plant, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties. When applied topically to the skin, these oils can help to calm and soothe the digestive system, reducing feelings of nausea and discomfort associated with motion sickness. Additionally, the aromatherapy benefits of the cream can also help to promote relaxation and reduce stress, further alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Angelica archangelica essential oil: This oil has natural antispasmodic properties, which help to calm the stomach and reduce muscle spasms caused by motion sickness.
Butylphthalide (BPT): BPT is a compound found in angelica root that has been shown to have anti-nausea and anti-emetic effects, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Furano-coumarins: These compounds have been found to possess sedative properties, which can help to calm the digestive system and reduce the severity of motion sickness symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the angelica plant have been traditionally used in herbal medicine due to their ability to calm digestive issues and alleviate nausea, making them an effective ingredient in medicinal creams for motion sickness.
Rhyzomes: Angelica rhyzomes contain a compound called bergapten, which has been shown to have anti-emetic properties, helping to reduce the symptoms of motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest 2 tablespoons of fresh angelica archangelica root and 2 cups of organic coconut oil for infusion.
Combine the harvested angelica archangelica root and coconut oil in a double boiler setup, heating it over low heat for 30 minutes.
Strain the infused mixture through cheesecloth or a coffee filter to separate the roots from the oil, resulting in 1 cup of herbal oil extract.
Mix 2 tablespoons of beeswax pellets with 6 tablespoons of coconut oil and 2 tablespoons of the herbal oil extract in a small saucepan, heating until melted and blended.
Pour the melted mixture into sterilized glass jars, allowing it to cool and solidify before use as a cream to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Sweet violet creams helps with motion sickness because they contain natural ingredients that have a soothing and calming effect on the digestive system. The sweet violet herb has been traditionally used to ease stomach upset and alleviate nausea, which are common symptoms of motion sickness. Additionally, the creamy texture of the sweet violet cream provides a gentle, non-stimulating coating for the stomach lining, helping to reduce inflammation and discomfort caused by travel. This natural remedy can provide quick relief from motion sickness without the use of harsh chemicals or artificial medications.
remedy_properties: Vasodilatory properties: Sweet violet cream can help to dilate blood vessels, which can improve blood flow and reduce congestion in the ears, nose, and sinuses, helping to alleviate symptoms of motion sickness.
Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds found in sweet violet can help to relax muscles and ease cramping, reducing the severity of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Sweet violet's anti-inflammatory properties may help to reduce swelling and irritation in the digestive tract, which can contribute to symptoms of motion sickness such as queasiness and stomach discomfort.
remedy_parts: Roots: Sweet violet roots are known for their calming and sedative properties, making them an effective ingredient in soothing creams for motion sickness.
Buds: The buds of the sweet violet plant contain a high concentration of volatile oils that can help alleviate nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried viola odorata flowers weighing 20 grams and grind them into a fine powder using mortar and pestle.
Combine the ground viola odorata flowers with 100 milliliters of cold-pressed sweet almond oil in a heat-resistant glass mixing bowl.
Steep the mixture for 30 minutes in a warm water bath maintained at 65 degrees celsius to facilitate infusion.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth into another container and discard the solids, retaining approximately 80 milliliters of liquid.
Combine the retained liquid with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of coconut oil in a heat-resistant double boiler set at medium-low heat.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Dong quai creams helps with motion sickness because its unique blend of herbal ingredients targets the root causes of nausea and dizziness. The cream's active compounds, such as ginger and peppermint, work together to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation in the body. By soothing the stomach and reducing stress on the inner ear, dong quai creams help alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, providing natural relief from queasiness and discomfort during travel or other activities that trigger nausea.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory properties: Dong quai's anti-inflammatory compounds, such as ferulic acid, help to reduce swelling and discomfort in the inner ear, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like dizziness and nausea.

Antispasmodic properties: The antispasmodic compounds in dong quai cream, including ligustilide, relax muscle spasms in the stomach and digestive tract, reducing symptoms of nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.

Vasodilatory properties: Dong quai's vasodilators, such as protocatechuic acid, help to dilate blood vessels and improve circulation, which can reduce feelings of dizziness and lightheadedness that often accompany motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of dong quai contain a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including polysaccharides and saponins, which help to stabilize and soothe the digestive system, alleviating symptoms of motion sickness.
Barks: The barks of dong quai are rich in flavonoids and terpenes, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest dried angelica sinensis roots extract by steeping one tablespoon of the root in one cup boiling water for 15 minutes.
Combine the extracted liquid with 6 ounces of coconut oil and 4 tablespoons of beeswax in a double boiler for melting.
Stir the mixture until it reaches a temperature of 180 degrees fahrenheit for 30 minutes.
Remove from heat and let cool before whipping with an electric mixer until it becomes light and airy for 5 minutes.
Transfer the cream to sterilized jars and store them in the refrigerator at a temperature below 40 degrees fahrenheit.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Skullcap creams helps with motion sickness because they contain scutellarein, a flavonoid compound that has been shown to possess anti-nausea properties. When applied topically, this herb is thought to stimulate the vagus nerve, which helps to calm the stomach and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, such as dizziness, lightheadedness, and queasiness. By applying the cream to areas such as the wrists or temples, individuals can experience rapid relief from motion sickness without relying on medication or other treatments.
remedy_properties: Scutellaria baicalensis (Baicalein): This flavonoid compound has been shown to have anti-inflammatory and antioxidant effects, which may help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness by inhibiting the release of histamine and other inflammatory mediators.

Scrophularia nodosa (Scrophularoside): The saponins present in scrophularia nodosa may help alleviate motion sickness symptoms by relaxing smooth muscle contractions, reducing inflammation, and improving blood flow to the digestive tract, which can help alleviate nausea and vomiting.

Valeriana officinalis (Valerenic acid): Valerenic acid, a sesquiterpene lactone found in valerian root, has been shown to have anxiolytic and sedative properties, which may help reduce anxiety and stress associated with motion sickness, making it easier for the body to recover from symptoms.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the skullcap plant contain a high concentration of sesquiterpenes, which are responsible for its sedative and anti-anxiety effects, making it effective in relieving motion sickness symptoms.
Rhyzomes: Skullcap rhizomes have been traditionally used to treat nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness due to their ability to calm the digestive system and reduce inflammation.
remedy_recipe: Gather high-quality dried scutellaria lateriflora flowers in sufficient quantity for desired dosage.
Measure and mix 20% scutellaria lateriflora flowers with 80% coconut oil in a clean glass jar.
Infuse the mixture by placing it in a warm location for 4-6 hours or overnight to allow active compounds to transfer.
Strain the infused liquid using cheesecloth or a coffee filter into a clean glass container and discard solids.
Allow the final product to cool and set at room temperature before transferring it to an airtight container.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Lemon verbena creams helps with motion sickness because they contain the essential oil citral, which has natural anti-inflammatory and antispasmodic properties. These properties help to ease tension in the digestive system and reduce inflammation caused by motion sickness. Additionally, lemon verbena's calming scent can also help to soothe the nerves and promote relaxation, reducing feelings of anxiety and discomfort associated with motion sickness. By using herbal lemon verbena creams, individuals can find relief from symptoms such as dizziness, nausea, and stomach upset.
remedy_properties: Astringent properties: Astringent properties help to reduce sweat and inflammation associated with motion sickness, providing relief from symptoms like nausea and dizziness.
Antispasmodic properties: Antispasmodic properties help to relax the muscles and ease digestive discomfort caused by motion sickness, reducing nausea and vomiting.
Carminative properties: Carminative properties help to expel trapped gas and reduce bloating associated with motion sickness, providing relief from symptoms like stomach discomfort and cramps.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of lemon verbena contain a high concentration of essential oils, which help to calm and soothe the stomach, making them ideal for treating motion sickness symptoms.
Seeds: The seeds of lemon verbena have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and vomiting associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh verbena officinalis leaves and stems in early morning to ensure potency.
Dry the harvested material using air circulation or desiccant method for 7-10 days.
Combine dried verbena officinalis (50g) with beeswax (20g), coconut oil (30ml), and lavender essential oil (5ml).
Melt the beeswax in a double boiler and blend with coconut oil and lavender essential oil for 10 minutes.
Allow the mixture to cool and thicken for 2-3 hours before whipping into a creamy consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Yarrow creams helps with motion sickness because they contain the potent herb Achillea millefolium, which has been used for centuries to soothe digestive issues. The cream's active compounds, such as apigenin and chamazulene, work to calm the stomach and alleviate nausea caused by motion sickness. By reducing inflammation and relaxing muscles, yarrow cream helps to stabilize the body's balance and equilibrium, allowing sufferers to feel more comfortable during travel or other activities that trigger motion sickness.
remedy_properties: Anti-inflammatory compounds: Yarrow contains flavonoids, phenolic acids, and other anti-inflammatory compounds that help reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness such as nausea and stomach discomfort.

Vasoconstrictive properties: The active compounds in yarrow, including achillein and sesquiterpene lactones, can help constrict blood vessels and reduce blood flow to the digestive system, which may help alleviate dizziness and lightheadedness associated with motion sickness.

Gastroprotective effects: Yarrow has been shown to have gastroprotective properties, helping to soothe and protect the mucous membranes in the stomach and intestines, which can become irritated or inflamed during motion sickness, reducing the risk of nausea and vomiting.
remedy_parts: Roots: The roots of the yarrow plant have been traditionally used to create herbal remedies for digestive issues and nausea, making them a natural fit for medicinal creams addressing motion sickness.
Buds: Yarrow buds contain a high concentration of flavonoids and phenolic acids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Gather and dry achillea millefolium leaves in a warm place for at least two weeks to ensure optimal potency.
Grind dried achillea millefolium leaves into a fine powder using a mortar and pestle or spice grinder, measuring out 10 grams of the herb.
Combine the ground achillea millefolium with 20 grams of beeswax pellets and 40 grams of coconut oil in a heat-resistant mixing bowl.
Place the mixture over low heat for 30 minutes, stirring occasionally, until the beeswax is fully melted and the mixture reaches a temperature of around 160°f.
Remove the mixture from heat and let it cool to around 100°f before whipping it with a hand mixer or whisk for about 5 minutes to incorporate air and create a smooth consistency.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Pot marigold creams helps with motion sickness because they contain the anti-inflammatory and antihistamine properties of calendula, which soothe the stomach and reduce nausea. The creams also help to calm the nervous system, reducing feelings of anxiety and stress that can exacerbate motion sickness. Additionally, the gentle, non-greasy texture of pot marigold creams makes them easy to apply and absorbs quickly into the skin, providing fast relief from symptoms without leaving a residue.
remedy_properties: Calendula's anti-inflammatory property: Reduces inflammation and swelling in the digestive tract, which can contribute to motion sickness symptoms like nausea and dizziness.

Pot Marigold's antioxidant activity: Helps protect the body against oxidative stress caused by motion-induced fatigue, promoting overall well-being and reducing discomfort associated with motion sickness.

Calendula's antimicrobial property: May help prevent or treat secondary infections that can occur when an individual is prone to nausea and vomiting, such as urinary tract infections (UTIs), which are more common in people experiencing frequent motion sickness.
remedy_parts: Roots: Rich in antioxidants and anti-inflammatory compounds, which help to reduce nausea and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Barks: Containing bioactive flavonoids that have been shown to have a calming effect on the stomach and intestines, helping to soothe motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest fresh calendula officinalis flowers and dry them using a food dehydrator for 24 hours at 150 degrees fahrenheit.
Combine one cup of dried calendula petals with two cups of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil in a clean glass jar.
Steep the mixture in a cool dark place for four to six weeks, shaking the jar daily, allowing the calendula to infuse into the oil.
Strain the infused oil through cheesecloth and discard the solids; mix in one teaspoon of beeswax and one tablespoon of vitamin e oil as emollients.
Whip the mixture until smooth and creamy, then pour it into sterilized containers for use as a topical cream to alleviate motion sickness symptoms.

remedies_list.remedy_desc: Hawthorn creams helps with motion sickness because of its unique ability to calm and soothe the digestive system. The herbal extract has been traditionally used to ease symptoms of nausea and dizziness, making it an effective natural remedy for alleviating the discomfort associated with motion sickness. By reducing inflammation and promoting relaxation, hawthorn cream can help alleviate stomach upset, headaches, and overall feelings of unease, providing a calming relief from motion-induced distress.
remedy_properties: Antioxidant properties: Hawthorn's antioxidant compounds help protect against oxidative stress caused by motion, which can exacerbate symptoms like dizziness and nausea.

Vasodilatory properties: Hawthorn's flavonoids and procyanidins dilate blood vessels, improving blood flow to the brain and reducing the risk of vertigo and lightheadedness associated with motion sickness.

Anti-inflammatory properties: Hawthorn's anti-inflammatory compounds reduce inflammation in the digestive tract and muscles, which can alleviate nausea, stomach discomfort, and muscle cramps commonly experienced during motion.
remedy_parts: Roots: Hawthorn roots contain flavonoids and oligomeric proanthocyanidins that help to calm the digestive system and alleviate symptoms associated with motion sickness.
Barks: The bark of the hawthorn plant is rich in flavonoids, which have anti-inflammatory properties that can help reduce nausea and dizziness caused by motion sickness.
remedy_recipe: Harvest and dry sufficient quantity of crataegus monogyna leaves to yield 50 grams of dried material.
Combine the dried crataegus monogyna leaves with 500 milliliters of carrier oil such as coconut or olive oil.
Steep the mixture for four hours under room temperature then strain and discard solids retaining liquid.
Mix the infused oil with 20 grams of beeswax and 10 milliliters of vitamin e oil, heating until fully melted.
Pour the hot mixture into a mold and allow to cool and solidify for at least two hours before use.

intro_desc: Herbal creams for motion sickness are natural topical treatments containing soothing herbs and essential oils that calm the skin and mind, providing relief from nausea, dizziness, and discomfort associated with travel or motion. These creams often feature ingredients like peppermint, ginger, and lavender, which help to ease stomach upset and reduce anxiety. For example, products like Sea-Bands and Bonine Cream contain natural herbs that work together to prevent vomiting and alleviate symptoms of motion sickness, allowing individuals to enjoy travel without discomfort. By using these herbal creams, people can travel with confidence, reducing the stress and anxiety often associated with motion sickness.
supplementary_best_treatment: The best way to treat motion sickness with herbal creams is to apply Peppermint oil topically to the skin. This helps to calm the stomach and reduce nausea symptoms. Another effective option is Ginger oil, which has natural anti-inflammatory properties that can help alleviate motion sickness symptoms. Apply a few drops of either oil to your wrists or behind your ears for fast relief from motion sickness discomfort.
supplementary_causes: Ailments similar to motion sickness that are treated with herbal creams are anxiety, stress, and restlessness. Herbal creams containing ingredients like lavender, chamomile, and valerian root can help alleviate symptoms of these conditions by promoting relaxation and reducing tension. These herbs have a calming effect on the nervous system, which can be particularly beneficial for individuals who experience motion sickness or similar symptoms due to travel, public speaking, or other stressful situations.
